subject: Project Management
title: DMAIC: The Five Steps
description: A micro-lesson that makes DMAIC: The Five Steps usable.

hook.text: Six Sigma projects follow five steps: Define, Measure, Analyze, Improve, Control. DMAIC.

buildup.text: Define the problem. Measure current performance. Analyze root causes. Improve the process. Control it.

discovery.text: DMAIC forces discipline: no jumping to solutions before understanding the problem with data.

twist.text: Most teams want to skip straight to 'Improve.' DMAIC makes them prove the problem exists first.

climax.text: DMAIC became the universal language of Six Sigma projects — a structured path from problem to solution.

punchline.text: Don't fix it until you've measured it.
